sessions.journal_text: Notes from the group project this term. Mobile app for a local charity. Four of us. I did the backend. It was a stressful but kind of rewarding few weeks.

turns.user: Last month my team of four built a mobile app for a local charity. I was the backend developer.
turns.assistant: Thanks for setting the scene. When you picture those weeks, what moment stands out most?

turns.user: We were behind schedule almost the whole time and scrambling near the deadline.
turns.assistant: Can you say a bit more about a moment when that scrambling was clearest?

turns.user: The night before the demo we found the login was completely broken and stayed up fixing it.
turns.assistant: That sounds like a vivid night. Would you like to move on to how those weeks felt for you?
Okay. Thinking back over that stretch, what feelings come up as you remember it?

turns.user: Honestly I felt anxious most of the time, and then a bit proud right at the end.
turns.assistant: What was happening around you when that pride showed up?

turns.user: When the demo finally worked and the people from the charity looked genuinely happy.
turns.assistant: Would you like to look next at what went well and what was harder?
When you weigh it up, what worked, and what felt difficult or unsatisfying?

turns.user: The teamwork went really well, but our planning was weak and the testing was difficult to keep up with.
turns.assistant: That's a clear contrast. Shall we look at why those things turned out the way they did?
What do you think was behind the planning being weak?

turns.user: We rushed into coding because we were excited, so we never really agreed on who owned what.
turns.assistant: That connection makes sense. Is there more you'd want to add there?
